commit.message:
feat(routing): update Azure LLM tier routing

- reasoning_heavy → gpt-5.2 (complex reflection)
- production_heavy → gpt-5.1-codex (complex coding/TDD)
- reasoning_light / production_light → gpt-5.1 (simple tasks)
- tasks_heavy → gpt-5.1, tasks_light → gpt-5-mini
- Was: tasks_heavy defaulted to gpt-5.1-codex (wrong)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: achieve 80% function documentation coverage (87.97%)

Add comprehensive GoDoc comments to 18 undocumented exported symbols in testdata/
directory, exceeding the 80% function documentation coverage target by 7.97pp.

Changes:
- testdata/duplication/below_threshold.go: AuthenticateUserByPassword (250 chars)
- testdata/duplication/small_blocks.go: GetUserID, IsValidID, CheckErrorA (200-250 chars)
- testdata/naming/bad_file_name.go: ExampleFunction (200 chars)
- testdata/naming/bad_identifiers.go: UserId type (220 chars)
- testdata/placement/misplaced_function/database.go: BatchProcess, CheckUser, VerifyUser (200-250 chars)
- testdata/simple/concurrency.go: 8 concurrency pattern examples (220-260 chars each)
- testdata/simple/interfaces.go: Any type (180 chars)

Metrics:
- Function documentation coverage: 77.85% → 87.97% (+10.13pp) ✅
- Overall documentation coverage: 79.16% → 88.31% (+9.15pp)
- Zero complexity regressions in modified files
- Duplication ratio: 0.4287% → 0.4284% (-0.0003pp improvement)

All tests pass with race detection. Completes Step 5 of PLAN.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: migrate JWT middleware from v2 to v3 (#400)

* feat: migrate go-jwt-middleware from v2 to v3

- Update module dependency from v2.3.1 to v3.0.0
- Migrate JWKS provider to use options pattern (WithIssuerURL, WithCacheTTL, WithCustomClient)
- Migrate validator to use options pattern (WithKeyFunc, WithAlgorithm, WithIssuer, WithAudiences)
- Update claims access pattern: use type assertion on ValidateToken return value
- Update error handling and API signatures for v3 compatibility
- Add issuerURL parameter to NewAuth() since IssuerURL is no longer exported from CachingProvider

Breaking changes in v3:
- Pure options pattern for all constructors (no positional arguments)
- ValidateToken returns (interface{}, error) with type assertion required
- NewCachingProvider now returns error as second value
- All tests pass successfully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update docker-compose.yml for keycloak binding

- Bind keycloak port 18080 to localhost only (127.0.0.1:18080:18080)
- Required for testing JWT middleware migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add comprehensive tests for NewCheckAccessToken factory function

- Test success cases: normal config, CacheTTL=0, empty ExclusionURLs
- Test error cases: nil IssuerURL, nil HTTPClient, negative CacheTTL, empty Audiences, nil Logger
- Verify typed nil handling with jwtmiddleware.Logger interface
- Use table-driven test pattern consistent with codebase style
- All test cases define complete behavior specification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: update context helper to use go-jwt-middleware v3 for subject management and add tests

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Merge pull request #5 from dchu3/feat/verbose-logging

feat: add --verbose flag for debug logging

Add a verbose/debug logging mode that surfaces tool call details,
MCP server responses, and errors to stderr. Enabled via:
- --verbose or -v CLI flag
- VERBOSE=true env var

This helps diagnose issues like opaque MCP server errors that Gemini
currently hides behind vague user-facing messages.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Merge pull request #4 from dchu3/fix/network-validation

fix: validate and normalize SOLANA_NETWORK in config

The MCP server expects "mainnet" or "devnet", but Solana RPC uses
"mainnet-beta" which is a common source of confusion. Config now:
- Normalizes "mainnet-beta" to "mainnet" automatically
- Validates the value is one of the accepted options at startup
- Fails fast with a clear error instead of a cryptic MCP server error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Port avatar customization logic from piclaw

- getAvatarInfo: resolve default app icon (/static/icon-192.png) for
  the default agent name, normalize avatar URL with trim
- getAgentAvatar: read avatar_url/avatarUrl/avatar (any key variant)
- updateAgentProfile: store avatar as avatar_url (consistent key),
  normalize with trim, compare before/after to detect real changes,
  cache-bust favicon on avatar change via Date.now() version
- DEFAULT_AGENT_NAME constant ('Vibes') used as fallback display name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Changed code elements from break-all to whitespace-nowrap with
overflow-x-auto. This prevents browsers from inserting newlines when
users select/copy text from the ingest key, webhook URL, and curl
test command blocks — which caused 'URL rejected: Malformed input'
curl errors.

commit.message:
Fix request body and response tabs merging into one tab group (#76) pymdownx.tabbed treats consecutive === blocks separated by only whitespace as a single tab group. After issue #35, the content-type tabs in request-body and the response-code tabs in request-responses were both at column 0 with only blank lines between them, causing them to merge into one tab group. Fix: add a

heading before the responses tab loop in request-responses.html. This provides a non-tab line that breaks the tab group chain, keeping request body tabs and response tabs separate. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add .env file support for credentials

Load TRELLO_API_KEY and TRELLO_API_TOKEN from .env files as a
complement to environment variables. Lookup order:

1. .env in the current working directory
2. ~/.config/trello/.env (fallback)

Environment variables always take priority over .env values.
The .env parser is a lightweight built-in implementation (no
external dependencies) supporting KEY=VALUE pairs, comments,
blank lines, and quoted values.

- Add DotEnv.swift: lightweight .env file parser
- Add Environment.swift: loads .env from cwd then config dir
- Integrate Environment.load() into makeClient()
- Update missingCredentials error message to mention .env
- Add TrelloCLITests target with 10 parser tests
- Update README.md and AGENTS.md documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Android Auto truncating podcast episode lists (#54)

* Initial plan

* Fix Android Auto not showing all podcast episodes

Remove the artificial 100-episode per-page cap in onLoadChildren and
the "More episodes…" FLAG_BROWSABLE item that were preventing Android
Auto from seeing the full episode list.

MediaBrowserServiceCompat.applyOptions() slices onLoadChildren results
by EXTRA_PAGE / EXTRA_PAGE_SIZE before sending them to the client.
The old approach capped results at 100 and added a "More episodes…"
item that was silently dropped by this framework slicing, making
episodes beyond page 1 inaccessible.

The fix returns ALL episodes as a flat list. The framework's applyOptions
then correctly pages through the complete list as Android Auto requests
each page. fetchEpisodesPaged(0, Int.MAX_VALUE) adds no extra network or
memory cost since the RSS feed is always fully downloaded before slicing.

Backward compatibility: the podcast ID extraction still strips any
legacy ":start=<n>:count=<m>" suffix so cached navigation items from the
old approach do not crash.

Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: hyliankid14 <1757523+hyliankid14@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve UNAUTHORIZED on tRPC mutations by reading JWT directly from request

auth() relies on next/headers which isn't reliably populated inside the tRPC
fetchRequestHandler context. Switch createContext to use getToken() from
next-auth/jwt, passing the raw Request object so the session cookie is read
directly. Server-side RSC caller keeps using auth() which works fine there.
